page.content: The table presents a detailed analysis of visual acuity (VA) measurements across different conditions. The headers include PPV, T&I, and P value, with the overall PPV showing 29/83 (35%) and T&I at 54/83 (65%). The first row labeled "VA, pre-endophthalmitis" indicates a sample size of n = 28 for PPV and n = 53 for T&I, with a P value of —. The mean visual acuity for this category is 20/55 for PPV and 20/50 for T&I, accompanied by a range of 20/20-CF for PPV and 20/20-LP for T&I, with a P value of —.

page.content: The subsequent row titled "VA, presentation" lists n = 30 for PPV and n = 54 for T&I, with a P value of —. The mean VA recorded is LP for PPV and HM for T&I, with a P value of 0.02. The range for this measurement is 20/80-LP for PPV and 20/40-NLP for T&I, with a P value of —. In the "VA, 3 months" row, the sample sizes are n = 27 for PPV and n = 39 for T&I, with a P value of —. The mean VA is reported as 3/200 for PPV and 20/390 for T&I, with a range of 20/25-NLP for PPV and 20/25-LP for T&I, with a P value of —. Finally, the logMARΔ is recorded as 1.23 for PPV, 0.57 for T&I, and a P value of 0.02, indicating a statistically significant difference.
